title: CloudFront Origins, OAC, SSE-KMS, and Private Delivery · Runtime Contract: S3 Origin Website Boundary
summary: A distribution points at an S3 website endpoint, retains legacy OAI by habit, permits the CloudFront service principal globally, and forgets the KMS key policy. Predict the representation, storage, trust, cost, and recovery evidence before inspecting a model. Planned artifact: Add and verify the s3 origin website boundary increment in private CloudFront-to-S3 origin contract. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Distinguish regular S3 origins from website endpoints by HTTPS, OAC, redirects, error pages, methods, and public-access requirements.
Model expiry-at-request-start, range continuation, key-group rotation, authorization changes, logs and referrers, cache behavior, and emergency revocation limits.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: S3, CloudFront, and Go Predict S3 Origin Website Boundary · Theory S3 Origin Website Boundary
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory Go file-delivery team handles case 6747 during an SDK and infrastructure canary. Build deterministic Go evidence to validate a regular S3 OAC origin with exact distribution and KMS authorization; do not treat an ETag, CORS result, emulator, or successful SDK call as stronger proof than it is; then change cache and retrieval cost assumptions and defend the new budget result. Browser code uses pure values and state transitions only. AWS SDK packages, networks, S3, CloudFront, KMS, IAM, WAF, databases, credentials, host state, race and leak checks, load, faults, restore, billing, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Distinguish regular S3 origins from website endpoints by HTTPS, OAC, redirects, error pages, methods, and public-access requirements.

Before reading, predict which action preserves the S3 Origin Website Boundary contract for the supplied data.
